mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.3pp unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2% year-over-year, sustained supply-side expansion supports corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of observations when industrial production growth exceeds 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% confirms macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery phase, any month-over-month increase in industrial production signals supply-side normalization that immediately boosts equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of observations during post-recession recovery periods when industrial production rises.
